column1: # ZAP Baseline alert rules for seedling-web.
# IGNORE  — not reported, never affects the scan result.
# WARN    — reported; zap-baseline.py exits non-zero, failing the CI job.
# FAIL    — reported; fails the CI job.
# Anything not listed keeps ZAP's default level (mostly WARN), so a new
# finding fails the nightly scan until it is fixed or triaged into this file.
# The scan targets the plain-HTTP seedling-web listener directly. In
# production seedling-web sits behind Caddy (see Containerfile.caddy), which
# terminates TLS and owns transport-security headers, so alerts about TLS and
# HSTS are out of scope for this layer and are ignored here.
# Triage new findings from the nightly report into this file: IGNORE for
# proxy-owned or genuinely-not-applicable alerts, leaving real seedling-web

column3: (Strict-Transport-Security header — TLS is terminated and HSTS set by Caddy)
(Suspicious Comments — false positive matching tokens in the minified frontend bundle)
(Non-Storable Content — the SPA shell is served no-store by design, see spa.delivery)
(Timestamp Disclosure — false positive matching integer literals in the minified frontend bundle)
(Modern Web Application — informational SPA detection, not a vulnerability)
(Private IP Disclosure — the 10.0.0.1 in the bundle is an example placeholder in a host-entry form field, not a real address)

(CSP unsafe-inline — style-src 'unsafe-inline' is required by the Emotion/MUI toolkit, which injects runtime inline styles and style attributes; the rest of the CSP is kept tight in code and guarded by a unit test)
